purpose: Visual scene plan, style cue, character refs, and image prompt controls for the Pallas Species Strikes branch-and-fold IF fixture.

visual_art_direction.base_scene_prompt: Inside an asteroid cavity maintenance yard in the Pallas Bloom Cluster: Service Ring Kappa is a curved industrial lane built against raw asteroid rock, with ribbed metal wall supports, scuffed deck plates, amber work lights, pipe-and-cable manifold panels, dry-operation cephalopod harness bays with humidity collars, oxygenation loops, pressure cuffs, limiter sockets, and many-armed tool rails, dry-side anchor tracks with hooks and safety tethers, engineered seal technicians in gray suits, industrial haze, worn yellow-white safety paint, and lived-in future infrastructure.

modification_prompts.prompt: Add workers halted in coordinated stillness at the manifold, with AU supervisor lights turning cold blue in the background.
Add a portable crawl-access bypass bridge glowing with temporary pressure lines between Teth's harness and the seal assembly.
Add security drones at the far hatch, small and sterile against the crowded maintenance ring.
Add a baseline rigger with a pressure-lash injury being steadied by coworkers, with emergency sealant and blood-dark coolant marks near the anchor rail.
Add an outside-feed recorder light reflected on Lio's slate, suggesting the Kappa Refusal has escaped the yard's internal board.
Show baseline riggers standing with anchor hooks at the front of the safety line beside engineered seal technicians instead of across from them.
Show Lio's slate displaying a marked crawl path with a false seam highlighted while Nara keeps both hands visible near the manifold.
Add a sealed incident packet icon split across AU safety, the yard board, and an outside Awakened Labor Front mirror on the slate display.
Quiet the scene into a post-shift maintenance ring: Orrin's cracked mug in the break rack, Teth patching a harness cuff, and workers repeating the manifold touch without open celebration.

visual_art_direction.legacy_summary_note: Compatibility summary only. Use visual_scene_plan for illustrated replay and per-section image generation.

visual_scene_plan.purpose: Illustrated Ink replay segmentation for website and imagegen use. Each entry maps a click-through Ink section or branch location to an imagegen-ready visual anchor.
visual_scene_plan.segmentation_rule: Distinct places, focal areas, object closeups, arrivals, legal/authority reframes, red-alert beats, branch locations, and aftermath states receive visual_scene_id anchors. If a new actor or institution changes the room's power geometry, stage the pre-arrival beat first, then give the arrival/reframe its own click-through and visual transition. Pivotal one-sentence beats may stand alone.
visual_scene_plan.prompting_rule: Prompts describe visible geometry, bodies, tools, lighting, material state, and stance without requiring the image model to know Aetheria lore terms.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Wide establishing shot inside an asteroid cavity maintenance yard before shift bell: a hollowed asteroid chamber with a huge curved raw-rock wall reinforced by dark metal ribs, scuffed deck plates bolted into the curve, overhead amber work lamps, wall-mounted pressure gauges, sleeping maintenance carts, folded harness frames, cable trays, worn yellow-white safety stripes, faint industrial haze, and no crowd yet; the infrastructure looks old, hand-repaired, and tired rather than sleek.

visible_characters.default_stance: environmental establishing shot; no named characters visible, only empty work infrastructure before shift

visual_continuity_notes: Service Ring Kappa remains the same asteroid cavity maintenance yard: curved mineral wall, worn yellow-white safety paint, hard industrial work lights, thin industrial humidity haze around dry-operation support equipment, dry anchor rail opposite the manifold, gray engineered-worker suits, and AU supervisor glass above the floor.

Maintain named-character visual identity from visual_character_refs for every visible named character; do not rely on names alone, and do not change body type, clothing language, equipment, or silhouette between scenes.
Use this as the broad location anchor for the fixture.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Wide-to-medium view down Service Ring Kappa, a curved maintenance lane inside the asteroid wall: on the left, dry-operation cephalopod work bays hold suspended dry-operation support frames with humidity collars, oxygenation tubes, soft restraint loops, rounded valve clusters, and many-armed tool rails; on the right, a dry-side rail of bolted steel track, anchor posts, heavy hooks, route bolts, and clipped safety tethers supports baseline human riggers. Along the rock wall, a glowing amber manifold panel spreads into pipe trunks, cable bundles, gauges, and route-diagram screens; engineered seal technicians in numbered gray suits wait near it while Lio walks through the center carrying a slate.

visible_characters.default_stance: walking the center lane with slate tucked under one arm, tired but attentive
near the amber manifold with other gray-suited seal technicians
at the dry anchor rail with a heavy hook and guarded posture
partly visible in a compact suspended dry-operation harness at dry-operation support equipment

scenes.base_image_prompt: Medium shot at the Kappa manifold wall: a waist-high mechanical spine of ribbed pipe trunks, bundled fiber cables, removable patch plates, amber route-diagram screens, pressure gauges, warning glyphs, and small hand-sized diagnostic touch panels bolted into curved asteroid rock. Nara-7 touches one ribbed metal access panel twice, pauses, then touches it with two fingers spread; three similar gray-suited technicians stand behind her in a precise line; Lio watches from the edge of frame with a slate.

watching from nearby without interrupting

scenes.base_image_prompt: Medium shot of the dry-side anchor rail: a long bolted steel track runs along the right side of the curved maintenance lane, fitted with anchor posts, route bolts, heavy hooks, clipped safety tethers, scratched warning labels, and old tool lockers. Orrin Dax braces a heavy dark anchor hook across his knees while his crew clears bolts along the rail; cheap recycler coffee mugs and patched gloves sit on a narrow fold-out break ledge; across the lane, engineered workers appear as distant gray figures near amber wall panels.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Medium shot of a cephalopod bare-minimum dry-operation support station in low gravity: a padded dry-operation support frame with humidity collar is bolted to the asteroid wall, surrounded by transparent oxygenation tubes, soft pressure cuffs, rounded valve clusters, flexible many-armed tool rails, small diagnostic screens, and life-support lung diagrams painted on a nearby panel. Teth Inkwise floats in the support rig while several arms manage valves and tools; one pressure cuff has thin padding and leaves a pale rub mark on the mantle. Teth's posture is controlled and stoic rather than theatrical, and the station is heavily patched, carefully maintained, and visibly built for productivity rather than comfort.

scenes.base_image_prompt: Aftermath wide shot inside Service Ring Kappa: the red alert has faded to bruised amber across the curved rock wall, and the Kappa seal assembly holds as bolted pressure housings, gasket rings, glowing tubes, and emergency valve plates settle back into safe readings. Workers remain in small tense clusters around the pipe-and-panel manifold, dry anchor track, and dry-operation cephalopod harness station; scuffed boots, tool marks, faint industrial humidity haze, slack safety tethers, and dirty deck plates show the physical exhaustion of people who won only enough to keep breathing.

global_branch_image_modifiers.prompt: Add a cold blue AU incident-log recording marker and workers halted in coordinated stillness.

visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue: Style: Media Molecule Dreams-style 3D volumetric brushstroke painting; visible sculpted fleck strokes; painterly VR-made geometry; every detail is made of brush matter that feels partly solid, partly liquid, partly gas. Extremely detailed but soft, handmade, and non-photoreal. Tiny brushstrokes are allowed and encouraged, but the broad composition must remain readable.
visual_scene_plan.prompt_assembly_rule: Assemble each image prompt from visual_scene_plan.global_style_cue, visual refs only for characters actually visible in the rendered frame, the scene base_image_prompt, and any triggered state_image_modifiers or global_branch_image_modifiers. Do not include visual_continuity_notes verbatim; use them only as tooling/review constraints unless rewritten as concrete visible prompt text.

visual_character_refs.purpose: Stable named-character visual identities for prompt assembly. Image prompts must include these refs for every named visible character; scene prompts alone are not expected to teach imagegen what Lio, Nara, Orrin, Teth, or Ilya look like.
visual_character_refs.prompt_assembly_rule: For each visual_scene_plan scene, include only the visual_character_refs for named characters actually visible in that rendered frame. Do not include refs for absent, off-frame, merely mentioned, or object-represented characters.

lio_vale.species_or_body: baseline human adult from Belt labor housing
lio_vale.stable_visual_description: Lean, compact adult human with medium-brown skin, short black hair clipped close at the sides, tired dark eyes, and a scheduler's stillness that reads as careful rather than passive. Wears a worn slate-gray AU maintenance jacket over a practical work harness, soft-soled magnetic boots, a utility belt with tags and seals, and carries a rectangular work slate with scuffed edges.
lio_vale.silhouette_notes: Usually centered between groups, shoulders slightly forward, one hand near the slate; visually reads as a tired worker-organizer rather than a heroic commander.

nara_7.species_or_body: engineered humanoid seal technician, BioDrone Standard labor line
nara_7.stable_visual_description: Slender engineered humanoid worker with smooth androgynous features, close-cropped dark hair, pale gray-brown skin with faint subdermal route-interface lines at wrists and neck, and a clean gray numbered seal-technician skinsuit with a small BioDrone Standard batch tag. Movements are precise and economical, hands often flat against rails or wall panels.
nara_7.silhouette_notes: Still, narrow, and controlled; frequently framed by the manifold or other gray-suited engineered technicians.
do_not_change: faint wrist and neck interface lines

orrin_dax.stable_visual_description: Broad-shouldered baseline human rigger in his late working years, light-brown weathered skin, close-cropped graying hair, rough stubble, and a pale frost scar along one side of his jaw. Wears a patched brown-and-orange dry-side work jacket, heavy gloves, magnetic boots, and usually carries or rests near an old dark metal anchor hook and battered recycler coffee mug.
orrin_dax.silhouette_notes: Solid, guarded, slightly hunched from years of rail work; visually tied to anchor hooks, coffee, frost scars, and old tools.

teth_inkwise.species_or_body: uplifted cephalopod dry-operation crawl maintenance specialist
teth_inkwise.stable_visual_description: Large uplifted cephalopod worker with mottled blue-gray skin, expressive dark eyes, and multiple flexible arms supported in a compact low-gravity dry-operation harness station with a bare-minimum humidity collar and oxygenation loop. The rig uses pressure cuffs, compression anchor loops, limiter socket, tool loops, and a body-support frame rather than human clothing; oxygenation tubes, valve clusters, padded supports, and curved tool rails make the body productive in dry cramped AU maintenance spaces rather than comfortable. One cuff is visibly worn and leaves a pale rub mark, while Teth's controlled posture suggests stoic endurance rather than ease.
teth_inkwise.silhouette_notes: Nonhuman, horizontal or suspended silhouette inside dry-operation support equipment; never standing upright like a human.

ilya_marne.stable_visual_description: Tall baseline human woman with cool light skin, severe silver-blonde hair pinned tight, sharp cheekbones, and a controlled corporate expression. Wears a clean dark AU superintendent coat with pale blue trim, polished magnetic boots, and a slim supervisor console cuff; often seen through glass or blue-white interface reflections.
ilya_marne.silhouette_notes: Vertical, polished, controlled, visually associated with supervisor glass, blue-white UI, and distance from the dirty floor.
do_not_change: dark AU superintendent coat with pale blue trim
seen through glass or UI reflections

visual_review_notes: Added a global Media Molecule Dreams-style volumetric brushstroke cue so image prompt assembly carries the intended non-photoreal handmade visual language without repeating it in every scene.
Corrected image prompt assembly: visual_continuity_notes are non-prompt tooling constraints, and assembled prompts include only character refs for characters actually visible in the frame.

Expanded pallas_nara_manifold_walk so the environment is self-contained; manifold line is described as visible pipe trunks, cable bundles, valve housings, patch plates, screens, gauges, and touch panels rather than assumed lore.

Visual reviewer full pass expanded all Pallas base image prompts so setting labels such as anchor rail, supervisor glass, crawl throat, seal assembly, dry-operation support station, and manifold are backed by visible geometry and materials.
Revised cephalopod visual and story assumptions: AU dry-operation support rigs are bare-minimum productivity equipment, not Navigator-style or cephalopod-native comfort habitat.

Visual scene plan added after review noted that illustrated Ink needs click-through segmentation, per-section base prompts, character visibility controls, and imagegen-ready descriptions of fictional equipment.
Visual reviewer pass added missing branch-02 visual scenes and aftermath modifiers for strike spread, BioElevate liability, evidence strength, Nara exposure/protection, and high security pressure.
Follow-up visual review returned accepted_with_minor_revisions; cleanup removed future-state modifiers from intro anchors and normalized ASCII possessives in visual prompts.
Added visual_character_refs so image prompts can assemble stable named-character identities instead of expecting imagegen to know what Lio Vale or other Pallas characters look like.
